adminPort.description: Port used for the Caddy admin API (health checks, metrics, graceful shutdown).

autoscaling.description: Autoscaling must not be enabled unless you are using [the High Availability version](https://mercure.rocks/docs/hub/cluster) (see [values.yaml](values.yaml) for details).

customMetrics.description: Additional metrics appended to the HPA `spec.metrics` list (Pods, Object, External metric types).

caddyExtraConfig.description: Inject snippet or named-routes options in the Caddyfile

caddyExtraDirectives.description: Inject extra Caddy directives in the Caddyfile.

ciliumNetworkPolicy.description: [CiliumNetworkPolicy](https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/security/policy/) for the hub pods. Use on Cilium clusters for features the standard NetworkPolicy lacks (FQDN-based egress, L7 rules, explicit deny). Independent of `networkPolicy.enabled`; enable whichever your CNI supports.

egress.description: Allowed outbound traffic. Pass-through to `spec.egress`. The DNS rule below is required when using `toFQDNs`: Cilium learns IPs by inspecting responses, so DNS visibility on `kube-dns` must be allowed first.

egressDeny.description: Explicit outbound deny rules. Pass-through to `spec.egressDeny`.

enabled.description: Enable the CiliumNetworkPolicy. Requires the `cilium.io/v2` CRDs to be installed in the cluster.

ingressDeny.description: Explicit inbound deny rules. Pass-through to `spec.ingressDeny`.

annotations.description: Annotations to be added to the deployment.

dev.description: Enable the development mode, including the debug UI and the demo.

existingSecret.description: Allows to pass an existing secret name, the above values will be used if empty.

extraDirectives.description: Inject extra Mercure directives in the Caddyfile.

fullnameOverride.description: A name to substitute for the full names of resources.

global.description: Global values shared between all subcharts
global.$comment: Added automatically by 'helm schema' to allow this chart to be used as a Helm dependency, as the `additionalProperties` setting would otherwise collide with Helm's special 'global' values key.

globalOptions.description: Inject global options in the Caddyfile.

healthCheck.description: Transport-aware health checks exposed via the Caddy admin API. When enabled, readiness and liveness probes use /mercure/health/ready and /mercure/health/live on the admin port instead of /healthz on the HTTP port.

httpRoute.description: Expose the service via gateway-api HTTPRoute Requires Gateway API resources and suitable controller installed within the cluster (see: https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/guides/)

parentRefs.description: Which Gateways this Route is attached to.

rules.description: List of rules and filters applied. When empty, a default rule routes all traffic with `timeouts.request: 0s` so long-lived SSE subscriptions aren't cut by the gateway (Envoy defaults to 15s). Mercure's own `write_timeout` (600s) drives subscriber rotation.

pullPolicy.description: [Image pull policy](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#updating-images) for updating already existing images on a node.

repository.description: Name of the image repository to pull the container image from.

tag.description: Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.

imagePullSecrets.description: Reference to one or more secrets to be used when [pulling images](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/#create-a-pod-that-uses-your-secret) (from private registries).

annotations.description: Annotations to be added to the ingress.

license.description: The license key for [the High Availability version](https://mercure.rocks/docs/hub/cluster) (not necessary if you use the FOSS version).

enabled.description: Enable metrics. You must also add a `servers` block with a [`metrics` directive](https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/options#metrics) in the `globalOptions` value. servers { metrics }

port.description: Deprecated: The port to use for exposing the metrics (use adminPort instead).

enabled.description: Whether to create a ServiceMonitor for Prometheus Operator.

honorLabels.description: Specify honorLabels parameter to add the scrape endpoint

interval.description: The interval to use for the ServiceMonitor to scrape the metrics.

metricRelabelings.description: RelabelConfigs to apply to samples before ingestion (sample relabeling).

relabelings.description: RelabelConfigs to apply to samples before scraping (target relabeling).

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which the scrape is ended

selector.description: Additional labels that can be used so ServiceMonitor will be discovered by Prometheus

nameOverride.description: A name in place of the chart name for `app:` labels.

networkPolicy.description: [NetworkPolicy](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/network-policies/) for the hub pods. When enabled with no ingress/egress rules, all traffic to/from the hub pods is denied. Supply rules to allow what you need.

egress.description: Egress rules (allowed outbound traffic). Pass-through to `spec.egress`. Allow at least DNS (UDP/TCP 53 to kube-system) plus the transport port.

ingress.description: Ingress rules (allowed inbound traffic). Pass-through to `spec.ingress`. Empty list + `policyTypes: [Ingress]` denies all inbound.

policyTypes.description: `policyTypes` for the NetworkPolicy. The chart always renders empty `ingress` and `egress` lists, so Kubernetes infers `policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]`. Set to `[Ingress]` to skip egress.

persistence.description: Switch /data from an emptyDir to a [Persistent Volume Claim](http://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/persistent-volumes/). /data is always mounted so modules writing under `caddy.AppDataDir()` (e.g. `rate_limit`) work under `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`. Enable persistence when /data must survive pod restarts (typical with BoltDB).

accessMode.description: A manually managed Persistent Volume and Claim. Requires `persistence.enabled: true` If defined, PVC must be created manually before volume will be bound.

existingClaim.description: a manually managed Persistent Volume Claim

storageClass.description: Mercure Data Persistent Volume Storage Class. If defined, `storageClassName: <storageClass>`. If set to `"-"`, `storageClassName: ""`, which disables dynamic provisioning. If undefined (the default) or set to `null`, no `storageClassName` spec is set, choosing the default provisioner.

podAnnotations.description: Annotations to be added to pods.

podLabels.description: Extra labels to be added to pods.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod [security context](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod). Defaults target the [restricted PodSecurity Standard](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/#restricted). `fsGroup` makes the chart's writable volumes (/data, /config, /tmp) group-writable by the rootless container; `OnRootMismatch` skips re-chowning PVCs on every restart. Override with `{}` if your cluster forbids these fields.

progressDeadlineSeconds.description: Deployment `spec.progressDeadlineSeconds`. A rolling update can spend up to `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` per pod draining SSE, so the k8s default (600s) trips `ProgressDeadlineExceeded` on healthy rollouts. Default fits a 2-pod rollout; scale up for larger `replicaCount` (roughly `replicaCount × (terminationGracePeriodSeconds + minReadySeconds)` plus a margin). Only applied with `RollingUpdate`.

publisherJwtAlg.description: The JWT algorithm to use for publishers.

publisherJwtKey.description: The JWT key to use for publishers, a random key will be generated if empty.

replicaCount.description: The number of replicas (pods) to launch, must be 1 unless you are using [the High Availability version](https://mercure.rocks/docs/hub/cluster).

resources.description: Container resource [requests and limits](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/). See the [API reference](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/workload-resources/pod-v1/#resources) for details.

securityContext.description: Container [security context](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container). Defaults satisfy the [restricted PodSecurity Standard](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/#restricted): rootless UID/GID 1000, no caps, no privilege escalation, read-only rootfs. Binding to :80 relies on `net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start=0`, set by containerd 1.5+ and cri-o. On older runtimes, set `service.targetPort` to an unprivileged port (e.g. `8080`). Override with `{}` to opt out.

nodePort.description: Set this, to pin the external nodePort in case `service.type` is `NodePort`.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account.

automount.description: Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials in hub pods. Defaults to false: Mercure does not call the Kubernetes API, so the token is unused attack surface. Enable for sidecars or modules that need the in-pod token.

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created.

name.description: The name of the service account to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

subscriberJwtAlg.description: The JWT algorithm to use for subscribers.

subscriberJwtKey.description: The JWT key to use for subscribers, a random key will be generated if empty.

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Pod terminationGracePeriodSeconds. Must be >= `write_timeout` so SSE subscribers drain at their own write deadline before k8s SIGKILLs the pod. Default = Mercure's `DefaultWriteTimeout` (600s) + 60s margin. Only applied with `RollingUpdate`; `Recreate` keeps the k8s default (30s) to minimize the gap between old pod gone and new pod ready.

tolerations.description: [Tolerations](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/) for node taints. See the [API reference](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/workload-resources/pod-v1/#scheduling) for details.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: [Topology spread constraints](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/topology-spread-constraints/) rendered into the pod spec. Evaluated as a Helm template, so it can reference `mercure.selectorLabels`.

transportUrl.description: Deprecated: The URL representation of the transport to use.

updateStrategy.description: [Deployment strategy type](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#strategy). Useful to set it to 'Recreate' when using BoltDB transport with persistence.
